title: rechsteineria trifoliata
side_effects: Rechsteineria trifoliata can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues in some individuals. In rare cases, prolonged exposure to its volatile oils can lead to headaches, dizziness, and nausea. Using Rechsteineria trifoliata for medicinal purposes without proper guidance can also cause interactions with other medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions. Furthermore, ingestion of the plant's parts can result in digestive problems, including diarrhea and stomach cramps.
